scope_note: QA Madelung d-Ordering Cert family [220]: atomic subshells (n, l) with n >= 1, 0 <= l <= n-1 identified with QA coordinates (b, e) = (n, l). Then d = b + e = n + l IS the Madelung quantum number. Claim: the aufbau filling order is exactly QA (d, -e) ascending sort. Verified through the first 36 subshells (Janet extended periodic table: 1s, 2s, 2p, 3s, 3p, 4s, 3d, 4p, 5s, 4d, 5p, 6s, 4f, 5d, 6p, 7s, 5f, 6d, 7p, 8s, 5g, 6f, 7d, 8p, 9s, 6g, 7f, 8d, 9p, 10s, 6h, 7g, 8f, 9d, 10p, 11s) with no mismatch. Selection rule: within-d antidiagonal step (b,e) -> (b+1, e-1) when e > 0; between-d jump (b,0) -> (ceil((b+2)/2), floor(b/2)) when e = 0. Derived: subshell population 4e+2 = 2(2l+1); period population 2*ceil((k+1)/2)^2 = {2, 8, 8, 18, 18, 32, 32, 50, ...} matching physical periodic table through period 7 plus Janet-predicted periods 8-11; shell-n total 2n^2. Distinct QA class from [217] b=e diagonal, [218] fixed-d hyperbola, [219] Fibonacci T-orbit: this is a d-ORDERING WALK across all d-classes. NOT a Q(sqrt 5) / Z[phi] structure — no phi, no Fibonacci, no quadratic form; polynomial-not-exponential per morning OB classification. Theorem NT guard: QA gives combinatorial backbone only; Madelung anomalies (Cr, Cu, Pd, Ag, lanthanides) and the physics of WHY atoms fill in this order live in the SVP/permissibility semantic layer. Source: Madelung 1936, Klechkowski 1962, Janet 1928. Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-13. Theory note: docs/theory/QA_MADELUNG_D_ORDERING.md. Checks MAD_1+MAPPING/ORDER/RULE/POP/PERIODS/SHELL/CLASS/SRC/WITNESS/F; 1 PASS + 1 FAIL; self-test ok
